title: Why Are There Fewer Sheep on Farms?
summary: Sheep are disappearing from farms in places like the Yorkshire Dales in the UK. For many years, families like Neil Heseltine's raised lots of sheep on their farms. But now, there are fewer sheep because it is hard for farmers to make money. Prices for things like food and fuel have gone up, and farmers get less help from the government. Also, people are eating less lamb and mutton than before. This means farms are changing, and some farmers are doing different work. The sheep help keep the hills green and pretty, so fewer sheep might change how the countryside looks. This could affect what we eat and how we enjoy nature.

people who grow food or raise animals on land
meat from young sheep that people eat
what farmers need to pay for things and live

questions.question: What animal is disappearing from farms in the UK?
Why is it hard for farmers to make money from sheep?
options: Prices for food and fuel are high
questions.correct_answer: Prices for food and fuel are high

questions.question: What are people eating less of now?
What might change if there are fewer sheep?

background_read: Sheep farming is an old job in the UK where people raise sheep on hills for meat and wool. Farms are places where animals live and food is grown. In recent years, things have gotten expensive for farmers, and fewer people want to eat sheep meat. This makes it tough for farmers to keep sheep, so they might try other work. The hills where sheep graze help keep the land green and pretty, so changes could affect how we see nature.
Article_Structure: WHO: Farmers like Neil Heseltine and families in the UK
WHAT: Sheep are disappearing from farms because it's hard to make money and people eat less lamb
WHERE: In places like the Yorkshire Dales in the UK
WHY: To show how farming is changing and what it means for food and nature

perspectives.description: Farmers are struggling with high costs and low profits, so they are reducing sheep numbers to survive.
